title: The WSJ Dollar Index Rises 0.29% to 95.86
summary: 1531 GMT – The dollar’s recent appreciation is driven by the potential for an improved U.S. terms of trade as oil prices rise due to the Middle East conflict, Commerzbank’s Michael Pfister says in a note.  Higher oil prices therefore boost the U.S. terms of trade as U.S. export prices rise relative to those in the eurozone, giving Americans more money at their disposal to spend.  The “decisive factor” is how long the oil prices increase lasts.
